title: Shared Birthday Check
statement_md:
Your class rep Meera keeps a register for planning birthday celebrations. Instead of writing dates, she copies the date-of-birth number printed on each student's college ID — year, month and day squashed into a single integer, so 17 March 2005 becomes `20050317`.

The register now has `N` such numbers, one per student. Meera wants to know whether a shared celebration is possible: do **any two students** have exactly the same number in the register?

Print `YES` if at least two students share the same date-of-birth number, otherwise print `NO`.
input_format_md:
The first line contains a single integer `T` — the number of test cases.

For each test case:
- the first line contains a single integer `N` — the number of students
- the second line contains `N` integers — the date-of-birth numbers from the register
output_format_md: For each test case, print `YES` or `NO` on its own line.
constraints_md:
- `1 ≤ T ≤ 10`
- `1 ≤ N ≤ 2 × 10^4`
- the sum of `N` over all test cases in one input is at most `4 × 10^4`
- `1 ≤ each number ≤ 10^9`

starter_code.python:
import sys

def solve_case(n, numbers):
    # your code here — return "YES" or "NO"
    return "NO"

def main():
    data = sys.stdin.read().split()
    pos = 0
    t = int(data[pos]); pos += 1
    for _ in range(t):
        n = int(data[pos]); pos += 1
        numbers = [int(x) for x in data[pos:pos + n]]; pos += n
        print(solve_case(n, numbers))

main()

reference_solution.python:
import sys

def main():
    data = sys.stdin.buffer.read().split()
    pos = 0
    t = int(data[pos]); pos += 1
    out = []
    for _ in range(t):
        n = int(data[pos]); pos += 1
        numbers = [int(x) for x in data[pos:pos + n]]; pos += n
        out.append("YES" if len(set(numbers)) < n else "NO")
    print("\n".join(out))

main()

hints: Comparing every student with every other student is N² checks — at N = 20000 that is a lot of work. What if you could remember, in one walk down the register, which numbers you have already crossed?
Put each number into a hash set as you walk. If a number is already in the set before you add it, two students share it — print YES. If you finish the walk with no repeats, print NO.
